title: Indulgent Smoky Polish Pork Dumplings
description: Rich and savory Polish dumplings filled with smoky pork and gooey cheese, served in a warm dashi broth, this dish is a perfect comfort food for a cold evening, and is also ayurvedic-tamasic-friendly due to its use of grounding ingredients like pork and cheese.

ingredients.name: farmers cheese
ingredients.notes: or other soft cheese like ricotta or cottage cheese

instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the ground pork, farmers cheese, salt, black pepper, and smoked paprika, and mix until just combined.
Gradually add the all-purpose flour to the pork mixture, and mix until a dough forms.
Wrap the dough in plastic wrap and let it rest for 30 minutes.
Divide the dough into 8-10 equal pieces, and roll each piece into a ball.
Flatten each ball into a disk shape, and place a tablespoon of the farmers cheese in the center of each disk.
Fold the dough over the cheese to form a half-moon shape, and press the edges together to seal the dumpling.
Bring the dashi broth to a boil in a large pot, then reduce the heat to a simmer.
Carefully add the dumplings to the pot, and cook for 10-12 minutes, or until they float to the surface.
Serve the dumplings hot in the dashi broth, garnished with chopped fresh parsley if desired.
